# FFmpeg (third-party)

**FFmpeg** is an independent project: [https://ffmpeg.org/](https://ffmpeg.org/). It is **not** authored by Jake Looper, and this project does **not** claim ownership of FFmpeg, `ffmpeg`, or `ffprobe`.

Jake Looper runs `ffmpeg` / `ffprobe` as **separate programs** (subprocess). The Jake Looper app itself is licensed under the MIT License; FFmpeg is licensed under the **GNU LGPL and/or GPL**, depending on build options.

## License texts in this folder

These files are copied from the official FFmpeg source tree ([FFmpeg on GitHub](https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg)):

| File | License |
|------|--------|
| `COPYING.LGPLv2.1` | GNU LGPL 2.1 |
| `COPYING.LGPLv3` | GNU LGPL 3 |
| `COPYING.GPLv2` | GNU GPL 2 |
| `COPYING.GPLv3` | GNU GPL 3 |

Which terms apply to **your** `ffmpeg.exe` / `ffprobe.exe` build depends on how that build was configured. See [FFmpeg Legal](https://ffmpeg.org/legal.html).

## Source code (for LGPL/GPL compliance)

- **Downloads and source archives:** [https://ffmpeg.org/download.html](https://ffmpeg.org/download.html)  
- **Git:** [https://git.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.git](https://git.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.git) (mirrored at [https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg](https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg))

If you redistribute FFmpeg binaries, keep these license files with them (or equivalent notices) and offer a way for recipients to obtain the corresponding source, as required by those licenses.

## Naming

Do **not** rename `ffmpeg.exe` / `ffprobe.exe` to imply they are part of Jake Looper or a different product. Use the standard names from upstream builds.
